objective: Prove that the H1-H5 humanoid proof chain can be rebuilt, replayed, recovered from hostile corruption cases, and inspected through stable identifiers with its exact provenance and visual receipts retained.
next_action: Implement the bounded engine-neutral H6 proof-chain module: reuse H1-H4 constructors, add one strict H5 decision record, derive an ordered aggregate chain ID, and prove deterministic rebuild plus hostile mutation rejection in memory before adding the disposable Kernel backup/reopen fixture.
research_gate: Refresh canonical and implementation evidence first. Use existing local fixtures and capability-free simulations before adding code. Browse only if a specific standards gap survives the local audit; do not acquire or import marketplace assets.
authority_lane: Owner-authorized H6 audit, capability-free fixtures, deterministic replay/recovery tests, and bounded local verification only. No asset acquisition, production import, runtime selection, promotion, approval, or protected-Kernel authority expansion.
context_health: Green: H5 is committed at e1b1404, capture is current, the full Forge gate passed, and H6 begins from a clean stage boundary with explicit macro and micro evidence.

macro_findings: F5 remains the active engine-neutral proof milestone; H6 must pass before any H7 promotion candidate exists.
H6 requires clean rebuild, replay, corruption recovery, stable identifiers, and retained receipts as distinct observable claims.
The normal research, case-by-case natural-method, macro/micro refresh, visual-quality, and cheapest-sufficient-simulation disciplines remain active.
H5 approved a visual target only; H6 must preserve that exact scope and cannot turn it into asset or runtime authority.
micro_findings: H1-H4 already retain exact source, representation, candidate, control, and artifact fingerprints, but H6 has not yet demonstrated one end-to-end recovery matrix across them.
The Kernel already has backup fixity, reopen/replay, corruption, and retained-receipt machinery that should be reused or adapted rather than duplicated.
The H5 visual target is bound to preview SHA-256 f3242d18b962103ec0b78fe424baf01db60c84d2c2a6f468c85d27818c145051; the actual marketplace file remains absent by design.
Windows live-process and SQLite-handle cleanup are known recovery-test hazards and must be separated from product failures.
The completed coverage matrix maps every H6 claim to existing proof, one exact gap, and the cheapest decisive next case.

visual_quality_gate.rationale: H6 tests proof-chain bytes, identifiers, replay, corruption handling, and retained receipts; it creates or selects no new visual asset. The already passed H5 pixel receipt is an immutable input to preserve, not a new visual-quality decision.

tiers_completed.result: Refreshed the program boundary and identified the five independent H6 claims plus likely reusable Kernel recovery machinery before implementation.
tiers_completed.evidence: MASTER_PLAN_V2 H6 definition, current stage context, backup/recovery and proof-receipt contracts

tiers_completed.result: Audited the H1-H5 contracts, implementations, receipts, and tests; repaired two stale H5 pre-decision statements; and recorded a five-claim coverage matrix selecting one bounded proof-chain module plus existing Kernel recovery machinery.
tiers_completed.evidence: docs/canonical-system/H6_HUMANOID_REPRODUCTION_RECOVERY_READINESS.md; 52 focused H1-H4 package tests and H1-H3 verifier scripts pass

simulation_ladder.unresolved_risk: Existing local tests may prove each subsystem separately while failing to prove exact H1-H5 linkage, stable cross-rebuild identifiers, or retention of the owner-bound H5 receipt.
simulation_ladder.expected_information_gain: The selected in-memory manifest cases will prove whether the one real gap—cross-stage identity and H5 decision continuity—can close without new persistence architecture.
simulation_ladder.estimated_cost: One small engine-neutral module, strict canonical fixtures, then one existing-Kernel disposable backup/reopen integration; no install, asset import, or graphics run.
simulation_ladder.regression_guard: Do not alter H1-H5 evidence bytes or identifiers; retain negative authority, rejected references, owner wording, and the H5 visual fingerprint through every replay or recovery path.
simulation_ladder.stop_condition: Stop expansion when every H6 claim has one decisive existing proof or one narrowly specified missing fixture; do not create speculative recovery infrastructure.
simulation_ladder.final_integration_gate: Pending: focused H6 fixtures, mutation-negative checks, clean disposable rebuild/replay, corruption recovery, retained-receipt verification, generated-projection checks, and the complete Forge gate.

unresolved_risks: Subsystem-local replay may not prove the complete H1-H5 chain reconstructs without identifier drift.
A corrupt or truncated receipt/link may be rejected without demonstrating recovery to the last known-good chain.
Generated projections may accidentally become recovery authority instead of remaining rebuildable views.
The approved H5 visual receipt could be omitted or semantically widened during reconstruction.
Windows file handles can produce environmental failures that must not be misreported as product corruption.
evidence_requirements: trace every H6 claim to exact current contract, implementation, fixture, and retained output
prove clean reconstruction from canonical inputs without relying on generated projections
prove deterministic replay and stable identifiers across a fresh disposable store or process boundary
prove corrupt, truncated, wrong-link, stale, and unknown-version cases fail closed and preserve a known-good recovery route
prove H1-H5 provenance, limitations, rejected evidence, owner wording, and the exact H5 fingerprint survive recovery
retain capability-negative evidence showing replay and recovery cannot approve, promote, import, or select a runtime
verification_plan: completed: audit H1-H5 contracts, crates, verifier scripts, and current receipts into a five-claim coverage matrix
reuse existing Kernel backup/recovery and ProofReceipt fixtures where their semantics exactly match
add only the smallest missing capability-free cases for cross-stage identifier and receipt continuity
run focused tests in memory or disposable directories before any repository-wide build
exercise corrupt, truncated, stale-link, unknown-version, and known-good recovery cases
run stage-quality, coherence, mutation-negative, projection, and complete Forge gates before H6 closure
resume_after: Continue from the H6 coverage audit; do not reacquire H5 visual sources or import the PolyOne asset.
